title: Deceased estates – supporting information
description: Information to provide with your objection or request about assets you inherit.
keywords: Capital gains tax, CGT, capital gains, deceased estates, inherited assets, private ruling

content_blocks.text: Check first whether your question is answered in If you are a beneficiary of a deceased estate .
To lodge an objection about an inheritance issue:

items: complete and submit the relevant form Objection form – for taxpayers Objection form – for tax professionals

content_blocks.text: To write to us to request an extension to the to the 2-year period to dispose of dwellings acquired from a deceased estate:

items: check first to see if you need apply - see Extensions to the 2-year ownership period and Practical Compliance Guideline PCG 2019/5 Capital gains tax and deceased estates - the Commissioner's discretion to extend the 2-year period to dispose of dwellings acquired from a deceased estate - if so, no request is required
submit your request, ensuring you provide all information required - see Request discretionary extension to sell an inherited dwelling .

content_blocks.text: To apply for a private ruling about another inheritance issue:

items: complete and submit the private ruling application form

content_blocks.text: Providing this information up front will reduce delays.
If you've previously given us any of this information, you don't need to resend it.
The information we need for a private ruling or objection about assets you inherit from a deceased estate includes:

items: the date and cost of the asset acquired by the deceased
a copy of the will and any resulting trust deed
whether the estate has been fully administered.

content_blocks.text: More information on inherited property and a series of questions that will help you find out if inherited property is exempt from CGT is available at Inherited property and capital gains tax .

content_text:
## How to lodge
Check first whether your question is answered in If you are a beneficiary of a deceased estate .
To lodge an objection about an inheritance issue:
- complete and submit the relevant form Objection form – for taxpayers Objection form – for tax professionals
- provide the following supporting information.
To write to us to request an extension to the to the 2-year period to dispose of dwellings acquired from a deceased estate:
- check first to see if you need apply - see Extensions to the 2-year ownership period and Practical Compliance Guideline PCG 2019/5 Capital gains tax and deceased estates - the Commissioner's discretion to extend the 2-year period to dispose of dwellings acquired from a deceased estate - if so, no request is required
- submit your request, ensuring you provide all information required - see Request discretionary extension to sell an inherited dwelling .
To apply for a private ruling about another inheritance issue:
- complete and submit the private ruling application form
- provide the following supporting information.
## Supporting information
Providing this information up front will reduce delays.
If you've previously given us any of this information, you don't need to resend it.
The information we need for a private ruling or objection about assets you inherit from a deceased estate includes:
- the date and cost of the asset acquired by the deceased
- a copy of the will and any resulting trust deed
- the date of death
- whether the estate has been fully administered.
## More information
More information on inherited property and a series of questions that will help you find out if inherited property is exempt from CGT is available at Inherited property and capital gains tax .
